name: PowerAdvocate (Wood Mackenzie) Energy Procurement API
what_it_does: PowerAdvocate (Wood Mackenzie) Energy Procurement REST API for utility and energy company spend analytics, supplier management, and procurement intelligence. Enables AI agents to manage procurement spend analytics and benchmarking for energy utility cost optimization, handle supplier performance and spend visibility for energy procurement management, access material and service cost benchmarking against utility industry peers for cost competitiveness, retrieve project cost estimation and capital program management for utility capital planning, manage supply chain risk and supplier diversity for utility procurement compliance, handle contract management and spend analytics integration for procurement compliance, access renewable energy project cost database for clean energy procurement intelligence, retrieve energy commodity and equipment price forecasting for procurement planning, manage RFP and bid management for utility procurement automation, and integrate PowerAdvocate with ERP, spend analytics, and supply chain platforms.
use_cases: Benchmarking procurement costs from spend analytics agents
Managing supplier performance from supply chain agents
Estimating project costs from capital planning agents
Analyzing renewable procurement from clean energy agents
not_for: Non-utility industries without energy procurement focus
Small businesses without structured procurement programs
Consumer energy without enterprise utility context
best_when: A utility, energy company, or large industrial energy buyer using PowerAdvocate wants AI agents to automate spend analytics, supplier benchmarking, cost estimation, capital program management, and renewable energy procurement.
avoid_when: ANTITRUST INFORMATION SHARING RISK: Automated peer benchmarking data sharing through industry platforms must comply with antitrust guidelines; automated sharing of competitively sensitive procurement pricing data between competing utilities creates antitrust (Sherman Act Section 1) risk. Procurement data confidentiality — utility procurement pricing, contract terms, and supplier data are competitively sensitive; automated data sharing via API without information barrier controls creates IP and competitive intelligence risk. Wood Mackenzie acquisition integration — PowerAdvocate was acquired by Wood Mackenzie (Verisk); post-acquisition product integration may have affected API stability and data coverage; verify current API documentation and product roadmap.

auth.notes: PowerAdvocate uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Boston, Massachusetts HQ (PowerAdvocate origin). Part of Wood Mackenzie (acquired 2018) which is part of Verisk Analytics (VRSK NASDAQ). Utility and energy procurement analytics. 500+ utility customers. Competes with Ariba SAP and Jaggaer for energy procurement analytics.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.notes: Boston, MA origin. Wood Mackenzie subsidiary (Verisk NASDAQ). Founded 2000. 500+ utility customers. Energy procurement analytics. Competes with Ariba for utility procurement.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; large analytics queries are async

known_agent_gotchas: ANTITRUST PROCUREMENT BENCHMARKING: Automated peer benchmarking data sharing must comply with DOJ/FTC antitrust guidelines; automated sharing of competitively sensitive pricing data between competing utilities creates antitrust risk; ensure PowerAdvocate aggregation methodology provides safe harbor

Procurement data confidentiality controls — automated spend analytics must implement information barriers; utility supplier pricing and contract data shared through PowerAdvocate without controls creates competitive intelligence leakage risk
Wood Mackenzie/Verisk acquisition integration — PowerAdvocate acquired by Wood Mackenzie (2018); verify current API stability and product integration within Wood Mackenzie platform ecosystem
No webhooks — spend analytics and procurement events require polling; no native push for spend alert or supplier performance threshold events
Large analytics query async processing — complex spend analytics queries require async processing; implement job status polling for large procurement analytics requests

Utility regulatory procurement compliance — utility capital and O&M procurement is subject to state PUC prudency review; automated procurement decisions must maintain documentation for regulatory review and cost recovery
agent_readiness.security_notes: Energy procurement analytics. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Utility spend and procurement data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Energy procurement. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Utility procurement and spend data.
